figures.caption: Figure 3. A layered SiQuAl output allows biological interpretation from global to focused phenotypic 547 analysis. 548 PCA1 and cluster analysis on the full feature set provide an initial multivariate overview of the dataset. 549 Based on this, SiQuAl calculates feature importance and performs a second PCA (PCA2) and clustering 550 on a refined feature set excluding low-impact parameters. To interpret the contribution of each feature to 551 the clusters reflecting phenotype, users can examine cluster-boxplots either on PCA1 or PCA2 alongside 552 condition boxplots with diverse statistical outputs. Together, this workflow guides the user from an 553 unbiased global overview to focused identification of the features driving phenotypic differences between 554 conditions and thus provide biological interpretation. 555

Table 1. Comparison of available open-source software tools to analyse spheroid assays. 591 592 BF: Brightfield; PH: Phase-contrast; FL: Fluorescent; Dim: Input image dimension; HT: High throughput; S: 593 Segmentation; SQC: Segmentation Quality Control; Time: Time-series analysis; Tox: cytotoxicity assessment; SA: 594 Statistical analysis; MPA: multiparametric analysis; SV: Segmentation visualisations; GV: Graphical Visualisations; 595 OS: Open-source. 596

Figure 2. Overview of SImBA-extracted quantitative features by feature categories. 537 Phenotypic features extracted or calculated by SImBA categorised into 5 categories. (i) Size and Growth: 538 11 metrics describing spheroid area and perimeter (later normalized to t0 reference by SiQuAl), and their 539 kinetic changes. (ii) Invasiveness: 5 features describing invasion morphology, single cell distance metrics 540 and invasion mode. (iii) Structural Complexity: 8 features describing (time-dependent) spheroid 541 architecture. (iv) Morphology: 4 global shape descriptors and (v) Cytotoxicity: quantified intensity of a 542 toxicity staining in the TSA. Full feature definitions are provided in Table S1. Numerical values are 543 reported in eight csv files in the SImBA Measurement folder (Table S2). 544

Figure 1. Overview of the end-to-end SImBA-SiQuAl workflow for 3D microtumour phenotyping. 530 Spheroid image sequences of growth or invasion assays are processed by SImBA including a unique 531 segmentation quality-control step (Prescreen), generating visual outputs and quantitative data for 29 532 spheroid parameters. The numerical output is analysed by SiQuAl through both single-parameter 533 statistical testing and multivariate analysis using PCA and cluster analysis. Together, SImBA-SiQuAl 534 provides an end-to-end analysis framework. Pink nodes: user input/choice, blue nodes: fully automated. 535

Figure 4. SImBA segmentation performance across imaging modalities and datasets. 557 (A) Representative SImBA segmentations on brightfield, phase-contrast and fluorescence images. Raw 558 image (left), binary mask (middle), overlay with SImBA segmentation (red contour) (right). (B) Stacked bar 559 plot summarizing the percentage of accurately (grey) and poorly segmented (red) images for each 560 microscope/dataset from the public SLiMIA dataset and datasets generated in this study (Table S4). (C) 561 Area measured by SImBA versus manually derived ground truth for 1,346 spheroids (red line: linear 562 regression fit; black, dashed line: Y = X). (D) SImBA runtimes in minutes (3 timepoints/spheroid) CR: 563 complete run, NP: no Prescreen, NP, NV: no Prescreen, no visualisation. (E) Example of SImBA 564 segmentation (red outlines) across a time series of an ECM-embedded invasive murine mammary 565 organoid (Cell Image Library RRID:SCR_003510). 566
